title: contrib: Add certificate generator for first-time contributors
body:
## Description

This PR adds a web-based certificate generator to the contrib directory to celebrate and recognize first-time contributors to the Podman project.

## Problem Statement

The Podman project lacks a way to recognize and celebrate first-time contributors, which is important for community engagement and making new contributors feel welcomed and valued.

## Solution

Added a certificate generator tool that creates personalized certificates for first-time contributors, including:

- **Interactive Web Interface**: Easy-to-use form for generating certificates
- **Real-time Preview**: See the certificate as you customize it  
- **Professional Design**: Clean, modern certificate layout with Podman branding
- **Downloadable Output**: Generate HTML certificates that can be printed or saved as PDF
- **Customizable**: Support for contributor name, PR number, and date

## Files Added

- `contrib/certificate-generator/certificate_generator.html` - Main interactive interface
- `contrib/certificate-generator/certificate_template.html` - Standalone template
- `contrib/certificate-generator/1stpr.png` - Sample certificate design concept  
- `contrib/certificate-generator/README.md` - Comprehensive documentation

## Technical Details

- Pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript implementation
- No external dependencies
- Works in all modern browsers
- Print-friendly CSS styles
- Generates self-contained HTML files

## Testing

Tested the certificate generator locally:
- ✅ Form inputs work correctly
- ✅ Real-time preview updates as expected
- ✅ Download functionality generates proper HTML files
- ✅ Certificates display correctly in browsers
- ✅ Print styles work properly

## Documentation

Comprehensive README.md included with:
- Usage instructions for both the generator and template
- Technical details and features
- Contributing guidelines

This tool will help enhance community engagement and provide a positive experience for new contributors making their first pull request to Podman.

```release-note
None
```
